---
title: "macOS Menu Bar App"
subtitle: "Swift 6 / SPM monorepo with NSPanel popover"
---

## Overview

The macOS app is a **menu bar application** (no Dock icon) that lives in the system tray. Click the `photo.artframe` icon to open an NSPanel popover with the KaosMaps glow design.

The architecture follows the **nano-speak** pattern: a two-target SPM project with local packages for maximum testability and composability.

## Package Map

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    subgraph "Root Package.swift"
        NanoMemeLib["NanoMemeLib<br/>(library target)"]
        NanoMeme["NanoMeme<br/>(@main executable)"]
        Tests["NanoMemeTests"]
    end

    subgraph "Local Packages"
        NC[NanoCore]
        NDK[NanoDesignKit]
        NIK[NanoImageKit]
        NSK[NanoStorageKit]
    end

    NanoMeme --> NanoMemeLib
    Tests --> NanoMemeLib
    NanoMemeLib --> NC
    NanoMemeLib --> NDK
    NanoMemeLib --> NIK
    NanoMemeLib --> NSK
    NIK --> NC
    NSK --> NC
```

## Key Design Decisions

### Two-Target Pattern

The `NanoMemeLib` library target contains all logic, while `NanoMeme` is a thin `@main` entry point. This allows the test target to import the library without importing AppKit lifecycle code.

```swift
// NanoMemeApp.swift — the entire executable target
import AppKit
import NanoMemeLib

@main
struct NanoMemeApp {
    static func main() {
        let app = NSApplication.shared
        let delegate = AppDelegate()
        app.delegate = delegate
        app.run()
    }
}
```

### LSUIElement

The app sets `LSUIElement = true` in Info.plist, which means:

- No Dock icon
- No main menu bar
- Lives entirely in the system tray
- Uses `NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)` at runtime

### Actor-Based Concurrency

All network and image operations use Swift's structured concurrency. `GeminiClient` and `ImageGenerator` are `Sendable` final classes with async methods, not actors, to avoid executor deadlocks with URLSession.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Lesson Learned
Using `actor` + `DispatchSemaphore.wait()` causes a deadlock because the semaphore blocks the actor's executor, preventing the URLSession completion handler from running. Solution: use `async/await` with `URLSession.data(for:)`.
:::
